data.title: Shenghui Cleanness Adds Major Shareholder-Linked Executive to Board
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Shenghui Cleanness Group Holdings Limited ( (HK:2521) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Shenghui Cleanness Group Holdings Limited has appointed Mr. Dong Jiangang as an executive director with effect from 26 January 2026, bringing more than 16 years of management experience across water utilities, intelligent equipment, and industrial technology companies in mainland China, as well as his current leadership role at Shanghai-listed Dong Guan City Huali Industries Co., Limited. Mr. Dong, whose affiliated company is already a major shareholder of Shenghui Cleanness with an approximately 19% stake, has signed a three‑year renewable service contract, will initially receive no director’s remuneration pending review by the remuneration committee and board, and will be subject to regular retirement and re‑election, a move that further aligns the company’s board composition with a key strategic shareholder and may strengthen governance and strategic coordination between the companies.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2521) stock is a Buy with a HK$0.92 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Shenghui Cleanness Group Holdings Limited stock, see the HK:2521 Stock Forecast page.

More about Shenghui Cleanness Group Holdings Limited

Shenghui Cleanness Group Holdings Limited is a Hong Kong-listed company incorporated in the Cayman Islands, operating through a group structure and backed by a substantial shareholder, Dong Guan City Huali Industries Co., Limited, which holds about 19% of its issued shares through a wholly owned subsidiary. The group is positioned within the broader industrial and services landscape in Greater China, with governance and operations overseen by a board of directors in line with Hong Kong listing regulations.

Average Trading Volume: 10,020,475

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

Current Market Cap: HK$1.72B

data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Ningbo Joyson Electronic Corp. Class H ( (HK:0699) ) just unveiled an update.

Ningbo Joyson Electronic Corp. has issued a positive profit alert for its 2025 financial year, projecting net profit attributable to shareholders of about RMB1.35 billion, a 40.56% year-on-year increase, with profit excluding non-recurring items expected to reach approximately RMB1.5 billion, up 17.02%. The improvement is driven by profit enhancement and business integration measures that have strengthened global operations and supported a recovery in overseas profitability, partly offset by around RMB160 million in non-recurring losses linked to the disposal of a weighing apparatus business and restructuring of certain overseas manufacturing facilities; the company reports no material uncertainties that would affect these preliminary, unaudited estimates.

More about Ningbo Joyson Electronic Corp. Class H

Ningbo Joyson Electronic Corp. is a China-based automotive electronics and components manufacturer, operating globally through multiple subsidiaries and listings in Hong Kong, Shanghai and Shenzhen. The group focuses on vehicle-related electronic systems and has been undertaking international business integration and restructuring to enhance profitability, particularly in its overseas operations.

data.title: Shanghai Bio-Heart’s Iberis Renal Denervation System Registered in Indonesia
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Shanghai Bio-Heart Biological Technology Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2185) ) has issued an update.

Shanghai Bio-Heart announced that its subsidiary Shanghai AngioCare Medical Technology has completed registration of the Iberis® Multi-Electrode Renal Artery Radiofrequency Ablation Catheter System in Indonesia, supported by commercialization partner Biosensors International Group. Iberis® is currently the only renal denervation product globally approved for both transradial and transfemoral approaches, a combination the company says can make RDN procedures safer, more effective and cheaper, underpinning its ambition to shift RDN towards outpatient settings worldwide and potentially strengthening its competitive positioning in international cardiovascular device markets, although the company cautions there is no guarantee the system will be successfully developed and marketed.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2185) stock is a Hold with a HK$6.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Shanghai Bio-Heart Biological Technology Co., Ltd. Class H stock, see the HK:2185 Stock Forecast page.

More about Shanghai Bio-Heart Biological Technology Co., Ltd. Class H

Shanghai Bio-Heart Biological Technology Co., Ltd. is a China-based medical technology company focused on cardiovascular intervention, developing innovative devices such as renal denervation (RDN) systems through its subsidiary Shanghai AngioCare Medical Technology. The group targets global markets, particularly the EU, Asia-Pacific and Latin America, often leveraging partnerships with international cardiovascular device companies to commercialize its products.

data.title: Hong Kong Johnson Discloses CEO Among Staff Arrested in ICAC Bribery Probe
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest update is out from Hong Kong Johnson Holdings Co., Ltd. ( (HK:1955) ).

Hong Kong Johnson Holdings Co., Ltd. has disclosed that its chief executive officer, Mr. Fung Chi Wang William, and two other employees—none of whom sit on the board—have been identified as the “Relevant Employees” whose duties and powers were earlier suspended in connection with an ongoing investigation. According to the Independent Commission Against Corruption, the three were arrested on suspicion of accepting advantages from subcontractors in exchange for the award of various commercial service contracts and contracts for the supply and maintenance of vehicles for the company, with the alleged conduct not involving any government contracts; the company has warned shareholders and potential investors to exercise caution when dealing in its securities and said it will issue further announcements as required under Hong Kong’s listing rules.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1955) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Hong Kong Johnson Holdings Co., Ltd. stock, see the HK:1955 Stock Forecast page.

More about Hong Kong Johnson Holdings Co., Ltd.

Hong Kong Johnson Holdings Co., Ltd., incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong, operates through subsidiaries under the “Johnson” group brand. The company is engaged in providing contracted services and vehicle-related support to commercial-sector clients in Hong Kong, relying on subcontractors for various service contracts as well as for the supply and maintenance of vehicles used in its operations.

Kwan On Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:1559) ) has issued an update.

Kwan On Holdings Ltd. plans to raise up to approximately HK$65.42 million through a non-underwritten rights issue, offering one rights share for every two shares held at a subscription price of HK$0.07 per share, available only to qualifying shareholders, whose holdings will be diluted if they do not subscribe. The company intends to use about 70.7% of the estimated HK$63.62 million net proceeds to repay group liabilities, 15.7% to support development and working capital needs for new construction projects, and the remaining 13.6% as general working capital, while controlling shareholder Sino Coronet has given an irrevocable undertaking to subscribe for its full entitlement and maintain its existing stake, with applications subject to possible scale-down to avoid triggering takeover obligations or breaching public float requirements.

The latest update is out from Youth Champ Financial Group Holdings Limited ( (HK:1160) ).

Goldstone Capital Group Limited has announced the passing of independent non-executive director Mr. Lu Zhicheng, who also served as chairman of the Remuneration Committee and a member of both the Audit and Nomination Committees. His death reduces the number of independent non-executive directors and committee members, causing the company to fall temporarily out of compliance with Hong Kong listing requirements regarding board independence, audit committee size and remuneration committee leadership. The board plans to appoint a new independent non-executive director and committee member, including a new Remuneration Committee chair, within three months of 24 January 2026 to restore full compliance and will issue a further announcement once the appointment is made.

The latest update is out from Lianlian DigiTech Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2598) ).

Lianlian DigiTech Co., Ltd. has formalized the terms of reference for its Board-level Audit Committee to enhance corporate governance and strengthen oversight of its financial reporting and control environment. data.description:

Lianlian DigiTech Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2598) ) has issued an announcement.

Lianlian DigiTech Co., Ltd. has formally set out detailed terms of reference for its Board Nomination Committee, a specialised governance body tasked with proposing candidates for directors, including independent non-executive directors, and senior management. The document specifies the committee’s composition, requiring three directors with a majority being independent non-executive directors, and sets out how members and the chairperson are nominated, elected, and replaced, as well as their required qualifications and tenure rules. By codifying strict eligibility criteria, clear appointment procedures, and safeguards for continuity of duties when members resign or become disqualified, the company is strengthening its board-level governance framework, enhancing transparency in leadership selection, and aligning its corporate practices more closely with PRC securities regulations and Hong Kong listing requirements, which is likely to be reassuring for investors and other stakeholders focused on oversight and compliance.

An update from China Galaxy Securities Co ( (HK:6881) ) is now available.

China Galaxy Securities Co., Ltd. has completed the first tranche of its 2026 public corporate bond issuance to professional investors, raising RMB6 billion under a broader debt financing authorization that allows issuances up to 350% of its latest audited net assets. The offering comprises two-year bonds of RMB1.5 billion with a 1.79% coupon and three-year bonds of RMB4.5 billion with a 1.85% coupon, with proceeds earmarked to supplement working capital, thereby strengthening liquidity and funding flexibility. Several lead underwriters and their related parties, including China CITIC Bank, CSC Financial, China Resources SZITIC Trust, and Everbright Securities, subscribed to portions of the bonds, while the company’s directors, senior management, and major shareholders did not participate, underscoring reliance on institutional market demand and reinforcing its position in China’s onshore bond market.

Jiahua Stores Holdings Limited has entered into a long-term lease agreement, together with related individual lease agreements, for a portfolio of properties to be used for its shopping mall, retail shops and other commercial operations, with the leases requiring recognition of right-of-use assets valued at approximately RMB177.8 million on its balance sheet. The transaction qualifies as a very substantial acquisition and a connected transaction under Hong Kong listing rules because part of the leased premises is owned by a company ultimately controlled by the founding Zhuang family, triggering reporting, circular and independent shareholder approval requirements; an Independent Board Committee and independent financial adviser have been appointed, conflicted directors have abstained from board voting, related parties will abstain from voting at the forthcoming extraordinary general meeting, and a detailed circular is to be issued to shareholders by 27 February 2026.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0602) stock is a Sell with a HK$0.04 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on JiaHua Stores Holdings Limited stock, see the HK:0602 Stock Forecast page.

More about JiaHua Stores Holdings Limited

Jiahua Stores Holdings Limited operates shopping malls, retail shops and other commercial properties in China, focusing on leasing and operating retail and commercial premises to support its core retail and mall operations.

data.title: Renco Holdings Given New Hurdles for Share Trading Resumption Amid Liquidation
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Renco Holdings Group Ltd. ( (HK:2323) ) has provided an announcement.

Renco Holdings Group Limited, a Bermuda-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong and now in compulsory liquidation, has seen its directors’ powers cease following a 2025 winding-up order, leaving joint and several liquidators in control of its operations and assets. The company has received additional resumption guidance from the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, requiring it to re-comply with various corporate governance and board composition rules, secure the withdrawal or dismissal of the winding-up order and discharge of liquidators, and publish all outstanding financial results while addressing any audit issues before trading in its shares can resume; until these conditions are met to the exchange’s satisfaction, trading in Renco’s shares will remain suspended, prolonging uncertainty for shareholders and creditors.

More about Renco Holdings Group Ltd.

Renco Holdings Group Limited is a Bermuda-incorporated company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, currently in compulsory liquidation. Its board powers have been terminated following a winding-up order issued by the Hong Kong High Court in August 2025, with the company’s affairs, business and assets now managed by joint and several liquidators acting as agents of the company without personal liability.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$50.31M
